✓ PASSEDShowcase: Retry with exponential backoff and hook tracking > retry flow > retries flaky endpoint with exponential backoff0.2s
com.blockether.spel.api-showcase-test.Showcase: Retry with exponential backoff and hook tracking.retry flow.retries flaky endpoint with exponential backoff
API TestingRetryExponential Backoffcriticalapiretry
Simulates a flaky endpoint that returns 503 twice before succeeding. Uses retry with exponential backoff. Hooks track all attempts. The Allure report shows the full retry lifecycle.
Show steps (1)
  • ✓retries flaky endpoint with exponential backoff
    • ✓Execute retry loopmax-attempts=5, backoff=exponential, initial-delay=10ms
      • ✓Attempt 1: GET /status/503attempt=1, status=503
      • ✓Attempt 2: GET /status/503attempt=2, status=503
      • ✓Attempt 3: GET /healthattempt=3, status=200
      • ✓Verify final result is successfulfinal-status=200, total-attempts=3
        • ✓expect: (= 200 (:status result))
        • ✓expect: (= 3 (clojure.core/deref counter))
        Final response
      • ✓Verify retry historyretry-count=2
        • ✓expect: (= 2 (count (clojure.core/deref retry-log)))
        • ✓First retry metadataattempt=1, delay-ms=10
          • ✓expect: (= 1 (:attempt r1))
        • ✓Second retry metadataattempt=2, delay-ms=20
          • ✓expect: (= 2 (:attempt r2))
      • ✓Verify attempt historyattempt-statuses=[503 503 200]
        • ✓expect: (= [503 503 200] (mapv :status (clojure.core/deref attempt-log)))
        Full attempt log
Attachments:Playwright Trace

Verifies the test server is healthy by hitting the /health endpoint. Inspects status code, response headers, and body content. Demonstrates api-step with auto-attached response metadata.

  • ✓performs full health check with detailed steps
    • ✓Create API contextbase-url=http://localhost:39379
      • ✓Verify context is ready
        • ✓expect: (some? ctx)
      • ✓[API] GET /health
        HTTP
      • ✓Verify HTTP status 200status=200
        • ✓expect: (= 200 status)
      • ✓Verify response is OK
        • ✓expect: (true? (api/api-response-ok? resp))
      • ✓Verify response URLurl=http://localhost:39379/health
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? url "/health")
      • ✓Verify Content-Type headercontent-type=application/json
        • ✓expect: (some? ct)
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? ct "application/json")
      • ✓Verify response bodybody-length=15
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? body "ok")

Shows how hooks intercept requests and responses. on-request injects an Authorization header. on-response logs status codes to an atom for verification.

  • ✓demonstrates auth injection and response logging via hooks
    • ✓Configure hookshooks=:on-request, :on-response

        HTTP
      • ✓[API] POST /echo with JSON body

      • ✓[API] GET /echo with params

    • ✓Verify hooks captured all requestsrequest-count=3, response-count=3

      • ✓expect: (= 3 (count (clojure.core/deref response-log)))
      • ✓Verify request methods loggedmethods=[:get :post :get]
        • ✓expect: (= [:get :post :get] methods)
      • ✓Verify all responses were 200statuses=[200 200 200]
        • ✓expect: (every? (fn* [p1__21383#] (= 200 p1__21383#)) statuses)
      • ✓Verify auth header was injected
        • ✓expect: (every? some? (clojure.core/deref request-log))

        • ✓expect: (= 200 (api/api-response-status resp))

        • ✓expect: (= 200 (api/api-response-status resp))
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? (api/api-response-text resp) "PATCH")
    • ✓DELETE — Remove resource
      • ✓[API] DELETE /echo — delete user
        HTTP
      • ✓Verify deletion succeeded
        • ✓expect: (= 200 (api/api-response-status resp))
        • ✓expect: (str/includes? (api/api-response-text resp) "DELETE")

API TestingStatus CodesHTTP Status Verificationcriticalapistatus-codes
Tests multiple HTTP status codes from the test server. Each status code check is a nested step with response parameters.
Show steps (1)
  • ✓verifies various HTTP status codes
    • ✓2xx — Success responses
      • ✓200 OK
        • ✓[API] GET /status/200
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 200 (api/api-response-status resp))
        • ✓expect: (true? (api/api-response-ok? resp))
      • ✓201 Created
        • ✓[API] GET /status/201
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 201 (api/api-response-status resp))
        • ✓expect: (true? (api/api-response-ok? resp))
      • ✓204 No Content
        • ✓[API] GET /status/204
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 204 (api/api-response-status resp))
    • ✓3xx — Redirect responses
      • ✓301 Moved Permanentlystatus=301
        • ✓[API] GET /status/301
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (some? resp)
    • ✓4xx — Client error responses
      • ✓400 Bad Request
        • ✓[API] GET /status/400
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 400 (api/api-response-status resp))
        • ✓expect: (false? (api/api-response-ok? resp))
      • ✓401 Unauthorized
        • ✓[API] GET /status/401
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 401 (api/api-response-status resp))
      • ✓403 Forbidden
        • ✓[API] GET /status/403
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 403 (api/api-response-status resp))
      • ✓404 Not Found
        • ✓[API] GET /status/404
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 404 (api/api-response-status resp))
      • ✓429 Too Many Requests
        • ✓[API] GET /status/429
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 429 (api/api-response-status resp))
    • ✓5xx — Server error responses
      • ✓500 Internal Server Error
        • ✓[API] GET /status/500
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 500 (api/api-response-status resp))
        • ✓expect: (false? (api/api-response-ok? resp))
      • ✓502 Bad Gateway
        • ✓[API] GET /status/502
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 502 (api/api-response-status resp))
      • ✓503 Service Unavailable
        • ✓[API] GET /status/503
          HTTP
        • ✓expect: (= 503 (api/api-response-status resp))
Attachments:Playwright Trace

✓ PASSEDRegression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split. Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath > writes only the path field (not the inline JSON field)0.0s
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split. Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..:storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath.writes only the path field (not the inline JSON field)
Show steps (1)
  • ✓writes only the path field (not the inline JSON field)
    • ✓expect: (some? path-val)
    • ✓expect: (nil? state-val)

Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state → setStorageState (inline JSON) > writes only the inline JSON field (not the path field)0.0s

Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). ✓ PASSEDRegression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split. Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > neither key > leaves both fields nil when neither key is present0.0s
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split. Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..neither key.leaves both fields nil when neither key is present

✓ PASSEDRegression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split. Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all. > :storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath > works for Paths with JSON-like substrings0.0s
com.blockether.spel.options-test.Regression tests for the `:storage-state` / `:storage-state-path` split. Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie. The fix: use an explicit key for each mode, no heuristic at all..:storage-state-path → setStorageStatePath.works for Paths with JSON-like substrings
Show steps (1)
  • ✓works for Paths with JSON-like substrings
    • ✓expect: (some? (read-field co "storageStatePath"))
    • ✓expect: (nil? (read-field co "storageState"))

Before the split, the library used a single `:storage-state` key with a fragile heuristic (`contains '/'` → path, else JSON). The heuristic mis-classified inline JSON strings like `{"cookies":[{"path":"/"}]}` as paths — which caused HAR recording to silently break because the `.storageState()` roundtrip embeds `/` characters in every cookie.
